1	# Story 2.6: Coexistence detection
2	
3	Status: ready-for-dev
4	
5	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
6	
7	## Story
8	
9	As a deepwiki user,
10	I want deepwiki to detect existing skills for the same repo/library before generating a new one,
11	so that I can choose to create alongside, merge, or skip — and never silently overwrite an existing skill.
12	
13	## Acceptance Criteria
14	
15	1. **Given** existing skills for library `foo` already present in the forge output folder, **When** `deepwiki` targets the same library `foo`, **Then** the user is informed: "Existing skill(s) found for foo: [list]", **And** the user is offered three actions: `[A]longside` (create wiki variant), `[M]erge` (update existing), `[S]kip`.
16	
17	2. **Given** the user selects `[A]longside`, **When** the pipeline proceeds, **Then** a new wiki skill is created with a `-wiki` suffix, **And** the existing skill is not modified.
18	
19	3. **Given** the user selects `[M]erge`, **When** the pipeline proceeds, **Then** the existing skill is updated through the US (Update Skill) workflow, **And** the wiki detection data enriches the existing skill.
20	
21	4. **Given** the user selects `[S]kip`, **When** the pipeline handles the skip, **Then** no skill is created or modified for that library, **And** the pipeline reports "Skipped: existing skill for foo".
22	
23	5. **Given** no existing skills for the targeted library, **When** deepwiki runs, **Then** coexistence detection completes silently, **And** the pipeline proceeds directly to skill creation.
24	
25	6. **Given** the pipeline is running in headless mode, **When** coexistence is detected, **Then** `[A]longside` is auto-selected (headless default — never silently overwrite).
26	
27	## Tasks / Subtasks
28	
29	- [ ] Task 1: Add coexistence detection section to step-auto-scope.md (AC: #1, #5, #6)
30	  - [ ] 1.1 Insert new §0c "Coexistence Detection" after §0 (URL Type Detection) and before §0a (Docs-Only Short-Circuit) / §1 (Load Context). §0c runs for ALL input types (repo URLs, doc URLs, and local paths) — coexistence applies to every deepwiki target.
31	  - [ ] 1.2 §0c invokes `skf-skill-inventory.py {skills_output_folder}` to load the full inventory. Parse the JSON output. If exit code is non-zero or the skills array is empty, skip coexistence detection silently (no existing skills to conflict with) and continue to the next section.
32	  - [ ] 1.3 §0c performs matching: for each skill in the inventory, check (a) `metadata.source_repo` matches the target URL (normalize both: strip trailing `.git`, strip trailing `/`, compare case-insensitively), and (b) skill `name` matches the derived target name (the project name from the URL path or the domain-derived name for doc URLs). A match on either condition is a hit.
33	  - [ ] 1.4 If zero matches: §0c completes silently, continue to the appropriate next section (§0a for doc URLs, §1 for repo URLs) without any user output.
34	  - [ ] 1.5 If one or more matches: present the user with the coexistence gate. List each matching skill with its name, version, and source_repo. Offer three actions: `[A]longside` (create a new `-wiki` variant), `[M]erge` (update existing via US), `[S]kip` (abort).
35	  - [ ] 1.6 In headless mode (`{headless_mode}` is true): auto-select `[A]longside` and log: "Headless: coexistence detected for {name}, auto-selecting [A]longside"
36	
37	- [ ] Task 2: Implement [A]longside path (AC: #2)
38	  - [ ] 2.1 When [A]longside is selected, set a workflow-context variable `{coexistence_suffix}` to `-wiki`. This suffix is appended to the skill name in all downstream outputs.
39	  - [ ] 2.2 The suffix is consumed in §6 (Build Scope and Determine Skill Name), §4a/§6a (multi-scope), and §0a (docs-only) — wherever the skill name is assigned, append `{coexistence_suffix}` if non-empty.
40	  - [ ] 2.3 Continue to the standard flow (§0a for doc URLs, §1 for repo URLs). The existing skill is untouched.
41	
42	- [ ] Task 3: Implement [M]erge path (AC: #3)
43	  - [ ] 3.1 When [M]erge is selected, emit an envelope signaling the forger to redirect to the US workflow for the matched skill. The envelope follows the existing pattern:
44	    ```
45	    SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"redirect","redirect_to":"US","skill_name":"{matched_skill_name}","skill_path":"{matched_active_path}","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","coexistence":"merge"}
46	    ```
47	  - [ ] 3.2 Write the result contract per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md` with `status: "redirect"`.
48	  - [ ] 3.3 Chain to {nextStepFile} (health-check.md). The forger's pipeline logic receives the redirect envelope and routes to US instead of continuing to BS. **STOP HERE — do not proceed to §1 or §0a.**
49	
50	- [ ] Task 4: Implement [S]kip path (AC: #4)
51	  - [ ] 4.1 When [S]kip is selected, emit a skip envelope:
52	    ```
53	    SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"skipped","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":1,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","coexistence":"skip","skipped_reason":"Existing skill for {matched_skill_name}"}
54	    ```
55	  - [ ] 4.2 Write the result contract with `status: "skipped"`.
56	  - [ ] 4.3 Chain to {nextStepFile} (health-check.md). **STOP HERE — do not proceed to §1 or §0a.**
57	
58	- [ ] Task 5: Update AN SKILL.md documentation (AC: #1, #5, #6)
59	  - [ ] 5.1 In the Stages table, add a note to step 1a: "Includes §0c coexistence detection — checks for existing skills before proceeding"
60	  - [ ] 5.2 In the Auto mode path description, add: "After URL type detection (§0), coexistence detection (§0c) checks for existing skills matching the target. If found, the user chooses alongside/merge/skip. Headless mode auto-selects alongside."
61	  - [ ] 5.3 In the Exit Codes table, document: exit code 0 also covers the "skipped" and "redirect" statuses from coexistence detection.
62	  - [ ] 5.4 In the Result Contract section, document the new `coexistence` field in the envelope and the `redirect` and `skipped` status values.
63	
64	- [ ] Task 6: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #5)
65	  - [ ] 6.1 Verify that when `{skills_output_folder}` is empty or doesn't exist, `skf-skill-inventory.py` returns gracefully and §0c skips silently — no error, no user prompt.
66	  - [ ] 6.2 Verify that the `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, and `maintain` pipeline aliases are unaffected (they don't pass `[auto]`, so step-auto-scope.md is never loaded).
67	  - [ ] 6.3 Verify that the interactive AN path (no `[auto]`) is unchanged — §0c is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true.
68	  - [ ] 6.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes.
69	
70	## Dev Notes
71	
72	### Architecture: Coexistence as a Pre-Flight Gate in Auto-Scope
73	
74	Story 2.6 adds coexistence detection as §0c in step-auto-scope.md — between URL type detection (§0) and the actual scope work (§0a docs-only or §1 repo-based). The change is a **conditional gate**: if existing skills match the target, the user decides; if not, the flow continues silently.
75	
76	**Current flow (Story 2.5):**
77	```
78	§0 URL Type Detection
79	  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit → health-check
80	  └── repo URL → §1 → §2 → §3 → ... → §11
81	```
82	
83	**New flow (Story 2.6):**
84	```
85	§0 URL Type Detection
86	§0c Coexistence Detection (all URL types)
87	  ├── No match → continue silently
88	  ├── [A]longside → set -wiki suffix, continue
89	  ├── [M]erge → emit redirect envelope → health-check (STOP)
90	  └── [S]kip → emit skip envelope → health-check (STOP)
91	Then (if continuing):
92	  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit → health-check
93	  └── repo URL → §1 → §2 → §3 → ... → §11
94	```
95	
96	§0c is a gate, not a branch — it does not change what happens after it (docs-only vs repo), only whether the flow continues at all. The [A]longside path continues with a `-wiki` suffix; [M]erge and [S]kip terminate early.
97	
98	[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#FR→File Mapping — "FR-21 maps to src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md"]
99	
100	### Inventory Script Interface
101	
102	Coexistence detection invokes `skf-skill-inventory.py`, an existing shared script used by 9+ workflows. The script scans the skills output folder and returns structured JSON.
103	
104	**Invocation:**
105	```bash
106	uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-skill-inventory.py {skills_output_folder}
107	```
108	
109	**Output (relevant fields for matching):**
110	```json
111	{
112	  "status": "ok",
113	  "skills": [
114	    {
115	      "name": "some-library",
116	      "path": "/path/to/skill/group",
117	      "active_version": "1.0.0",
118	      "active_path": "/path/to/active/version",
119	      "metadata": {
120	        "source_repo": "https://github.com/org/some-library",
121	        "version": "1.0.0",
122	        "language": "typescript"
123	      }
124	    }
125	  ]
126	}
127	```
128	
129	**Exit codes:** 0 = success (may have zero skills), 1 = error (directory not found, etc.)
130	
131	The script already exists at `src/shared/scripts/skf-skill-inventory.py`. No modifications needed to the script itself — it already returns `metadata.source_repo` which is the primary matching key.
132	
133	[Source: src/shared/scripts/skf-skill-inventory.py — full script, 210 lines]
134	
135	### Matching Logic
136	
137	The matching must be robust against URL format variations:
138	
139	| Target Input | Existing `source_repo` | Should Match? |
140	|---|---|---|
141	| `https://github.com/org/foo` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (exact) |
142	| `https://github.com/org/foo.git` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (strip `.git`) |
143	| `https://github.com/org/foo/` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (strip trailing `/`) |
144	| `https://GITHUB.com/ORG/foo` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (case-insensitive) |
145	| `https://github.com/org/foo` | `https://github.com/other-org/foo` | No (different org) |
146	| `https://docs.foo.io` | skill named `docs-foo-io` | Yes (name match for doc URLs) |
147	
148	**Two match conditions (either triggers a hit):**
149	1. **URL match:** Normalize both the target and each skill's `source_repo` — strip scheme, strip trailing `.git`, strip trailing `/`, lowercase — then compare.
150	2. **Name match:** Derive the expected skill name from the target (same logic as §6 for repo URLs, §0a for doc URLs), then compare against each skill's `name`.
151	
152	Both normalizations happen in the step file (string manipulation), not in a Python script — the logic is simple enough for step-file-level pattern matching.
153	
154	### Coexistence Gate UI
155	
156	When matches are found, present:
157	
158	```
159	⚠️ Existing skill(s) found for {target_name}:
160	
161	  • {skill_name} (v{version}) — source: {source_repo}
162	  [repeat for each match]
163	
164	Actions:
165	  [A]longside — Create a new wiki skill with "-wiki" suffix (existing skill untouched)
166	  [M]erge     — Update the existing skill via US workflow (wiki data enriches it)
167	  [S]kip      — Do not create or modify any skill for this library
168	
169	Choose [A/M/S]:
170	```
171	
172	In headless mode, auto-select `[A]` and log the decision.
173	
174	### -wiki Suffix Mechanics
175	
176	When [A]longside is selected, the `{coexistence_suffix}` variable is set to `-wiki`. This suffix is consumed at skill name assignment points:
177	
178	| Section | Current Name Assignment | With Suffix |
179	|---|---|---|
180	| §0a (docs-only) | `skill_name` = domain-derived (e.g., `docs-example-com`) | `docs-example-com-wiki` |
181	| §6 (single-scope) | `skill_name` = manifest name or directory name | `{name}-wiki` |
182	| §4a/§6a (multi-scope) | `skill_name` = `{project}-{package}` | `{project}-{package}-wiki` |
183	
184	The suffix applies to:
185	- The `name` field in skill-brief.yaml
186	- The forge-data subdirectory name (`{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}-wiki/`)
187	- The analysis report `confirmed_units[].name`
188	
189	The suffix does NOT apply to:
190	- The `source_repo` field (still points to the original repo)
191	- Include/exclude patterns (still scope the original source)
192	- The shape detection or scope mapping (shape is a property of the source, not the skill)
193	
194	### [M]erge Path: Redirect to US
195	
196	The [M]erge path introduces a new `redirect` status in the AN envelope. The forger's pipeline logic already handles status values — it checks for `"success"` and `"error"`. A `"redirect"` status signals the forger to substitute the next workflow in the chain.
197	
198	**Forger behavior on receiving `status: "redirect"`:**
199	- The `redirect_to` field names the target workflow code (`US`)
200	- The `skill_name` field identifies which skill US should operate on
201	- The forger replaces the remaining pipeline chain with `US TS EX` (update → test → export)
202	- This is analogous to the existing `AS → US` skip logic: "if severity is CLEAN, skip US"
203	
204	The redirect is a **step-file-level instruction** — step-auto-scope.md emits the envelope, and the forger's existing pipeline orchestration interprets it. No new forger code is needed beyond recognizing `"redirect"` in the status field.
205	
206	**Important:** The [M]erge path does NOT directly invoke US. It emits the redirect envelope and chains to health-check. The forger reads the envelope and handles routing. This preserves the pipeline's left-to-right, one-workflow-at-a-time model.
207	
208	[Source: src/shared/references/pipeline-contracts.md — "Data Flow" table, forger routes based on result status]
209	[Source: src/skf-forger/SKILL.md:105 — "AS followed by US: if summary.severity is CLEAN, skip US"]
210	
211	### [S]kip Path: Clean Termination
212	
213	The [S]kip path uses the existing `"skipped"` status pattern (analogous to how the forger skips US when AS reports CLEAN). The envelope `unit_counts.skipped: 1` signals that one unit was intentionally skipped. The forger treats this as a successful termination — no error, no downstream workflows executed.
214	
215	### Headless Gate Convention
216	
217	Per the established convention (`headless-gate-convention.md`, stories 2.3/2.5):
218	- Coexistence gate is a **Confirm Gate [C]** with a default action
219	- Default action: `[A]longside` (safest — creates new skill, preserves existing)
220	- In headless mode: auto-select default and log the auto-decision
221	- The gate does NOT auto-select [M]erge (destructive — modifies existing) or [S]kip (lossy — produces nothing)
222	
223	[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md — headless gate pattern reference]
224	
225	### Project Structure Notes
226	
227	- No new files created — this story modifies existing step files in `src/skf-analyze-source/references/` and `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md`
228	- No new scripts — `skf-skill-inventory.py` already exists and returns all needed data
229	- No new test files — step files are validated structurally via `npm test`, not with pytest
230	- The matching logic (URL normalization + name comparison) is step-file-level string manipulation, not a Python script
231	
232	### Previous Story Intelligence
233	
234	**Story 2.5 patterns to replicate:**
235	
236	1. **Section numbering preserves backward compatibility.** Story 2.5 inserted §0/§0a before §1 without renumbering. Story 2.6 inserts §0c between §0 and §0a/§1 — no renumbering of existing sections.
237	
238	2. **Short-circuit paths chain to health-check.** Both §0a (docs-only) and story 2.6's [M]erge/[S]kip paths chain to {nextStepFile} after emitting their envelopes. The pattern is: emit envelope → write result contract → chain to health-check.
239	
240	3. **Envelope extension, not creation.** Story 2.5 added `source_type` to the existing envelope. Story 2.6 adds `coexistence` field and two new status values (`redirect`, `skipped`). Backward-compatible — existing consumers check for `"success"` and `"error"`, ignoring unknown statuses.
241	
242	4. **Headless auto-proceed with default action.** Same pattern as step-auto-validate.md's `[A]pprove` default.
243	
244	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-5-docs-only-deepwiki-path.md#Dev Notes]
245	
246	**Story 2.4 patterns to replicate:**
247	
248	1. **Conditional branching preserves full backward compatibility.** §0c is a gate that either continues silently or terminates early. The entire flow from §0a through §11 is unchanged when no match is found.
249	
250	[Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/2-5-docs-only-deepwiki-path.md#Previous Story Intelligence]
251	
252	### Git Intelligence
253	
254	Recent commits on `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` branch:
255	- `7ef81ee` feat(story-2.5): Docs-only deepwiki path
256	- `46ddc84` feat(story-2.4): Auto-decomposition for massive repos
257	- `50968ee` feat(story-2.3): Auto-brief validation with progressive fallback
258	- `06652e9` feat(story-2.2): Auto-brief generation for BS
259	- `9d1adc5` feat(story-2.1): Auto-scope mode for AN
260	
261	Stories 2.1→2.5 committed sequentially. Story 2.6 continues this pattern.
262	
263	### Git Workflow
264	
265	- **Branch**: `deepwiki-zero-ceremony-skill-creation` (epic-level branch for all Epic 2 stories)
266	- **Commit onto**: This branch, next sequential commit after story 2.5
267	- **PR target**: `main` (single PR when Epic 2 is complete)
268	
269	### Dependencies
270	
271	| Dependency | Story | Status | What This Story Needs |
272	|-----------|-------|--------|----------------------|
273	| `step-auto-scope.md` | 2.1, 2.4, 2.5 | done | The file being modified — coexistence detection inserted between §0 and §0a/§1 |
274	| `skf-skill-inventory.py` | pre-existing | done | Inventory script — invoked to find existing skills. Returns metadata.source_repo for URL matching |
275	| `pipeline-contracts.md` | pre-existing | done | Reference for envelope format and forger routing behavior |
276	| `skf-forger/SKILL.md` | pre-existing | done | Pipeline mode infrastructure — understands `status: "redirect"` pattern (analogous to AS→US skip) |
277	
278	No blocking dependencies. All prerequisite scripts and step files are complete.
279	
280	### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
281	
282	1. **Do NOT modify `skf-skill-inventory.py`.** The script already returns all needed fields. Adding coexistence-specific logic to the shared script would couple it to the deepwiki pipeline. Matching logic belongs in the step file.
283	
284	2. **Do NOT invoke US directly from step-auto-scope.md.** The [M]erge path emits a redirect envelope — the forger handles routing. Direct invocation would break the pipeline's left-to-right model.
285	
286	3. **Do NOT overwrite existing skills in any path.** [A]longside creates a new skill with `-wiki` suffix. [M]erge delegates to US. [S]kip does nothing. The entire point of FR-21 is "never silently overwrite."
287	
288	4. **Do NOT run coexistence detection in interactive AN mode.** §0c is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true (i.e., within the deepwiki pipeline). Interactive AN users manually choose their targets.
289	
290	5. **Do NOT make coexistence detection blocking when inventory fails.** If `skf-skill-inventory.py` returns an error (skills dir doesn't exist, JSON parse error), skip detection silently. This is a pre-flight check, not a hard gate — failure to check is not a reason to halt the pipeline.
291	
292	6. **Do NOT add the `-wiki` suffix to the `source_repo` field.** The source repo is the actual repo/URL. The suffix only applies to the skill name and derived paths.
293	
294	7. **Do NOT renumber existing sections (§0, §0a, §1-§11).** Insert §0c as a new section without changing the numbering of existing sections. This matches the pattern from stories 2.4 and 2.5.
295	
296	### Files to Modify

298	| File | Change |

300	| `src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md` | Insert §0c (Coexistence Detection) between §0 and §0a/§1; add `-wiki` suffix application at §0a §4, §6, §4a/§6a name assignment points |
301	| `src/skf-analyze-source/SKILL.md` | Update Stages table, auto mode path, exit codes, and result contract for coexistence detection |
302

# Story 2.6: Coexistence detection

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a deepwiki user,
I want deepwiki to detect existing skills for the same repo/library before generating a new one,
so that I can choose to create alongside, merge, or skip — and never silently overwrite an existing skill.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** existing skills for library `foo` already present in the forge output folder, **When** `deepwiki` targets the same library `foo`, **Then** the user is informed: "Existing skill(s) found for foo: [list]", **And** the user is offered three actions: `[A]longside` (create wiki variant), `[M]erge` (update existing), `[S]kip`.

2. **Given** the user selects `[A]longside`, **When** the pipeline proceeds, **Then** a new wiki skill is created with a `-wiki` suffix, **And** the existing skill is not modified.

3. **Given** the user selects `[M]erge`, **When** the pipeline proceeds, **Then** the existing skill is updated through the US (Update Skill) workflow, **And** the wiki detection data enriches the existing skill.

4. **Given** the user selects `[S]kip`, **When** the pipeline handles the skip, **Then** no skill is created or modified for that library, **And** the pipeline reports "Skipped: existing skill for foo".

5. **Given** no existing skills for the targeted library, **When** deepwiki runs, **Then** coexistence detection completes silently, **And** the pipeline proceeds directly to skill creation.

6. **Given** the pipeline is running in headless mode, **When** coexistence is detected, **Then** `[A]longside` is auto-selected (headless default — never silently overwrite).

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Add coexistence detection section to step-auto-scope.md (AC: #1, #5, #6)
  - [ ] 1.1 Insert new §0c "Coexistence Detection" after §0 (URL Type Detection) and before §0a (Docs-Only Short-Circuit) / §1 (Load Context). §0c runs for ALL input types (repo URLs, doc URLs, and local paths) — coexistence applies to every deepwiki target.
  - [ ] 1.2 §0c invokes `skf-skill-inventory.py {skills_output_folder}` to load the full inventory. Parse the JSON output. If exit code is non-zero or the skills array is empty, skip coexistence detection silently (no existing skills to conflict with) and continue to the next section.
  - [ ] 1.3 §0c performs matching: for each skill in the inventory, check (a) `metadata.source_repo` matches the target URL (normalize both: strip trailing `.git`, strip trailing `/`, compare case-insensitively), and (b) skill `name` matches the derived target name (the project name from the URL path or the domain-derived name for doc URLs). A match on either condition is a hit.
  - [ ] 1.4 If zero matches: §0c completes silently, continue to the appropriate next section (§0a for doc URLs, §1 for repo URLs) without any user output.
  - [ ] 1.5 If one or more matches: present the user with the coexistence gate. List each matching skill with its name, version, and source_repo. Offer three actions: `[A]longside` (create a new `-wiki` variant), `[M]erge` (update existing via US), `[S]kip` (abort).
  - [ ] 1.6 In headless mode (`{headless_mode}` is true): auto-select `[A]longside` and log: "Headless: coexistence detected for {name}, auto-selecting [A]longside"

- [ ] Task 2: Implement [A]longside path (AC: #2)
  - [ ] 2.1 When [A]longside is selected, set a workflow-context variable `{coexistence_suffix}` to `-wiki`. This suffix is appended to the skill name in all downstream outputs.
  - [ ] 2.2 The suffix is consumed in §6 (Build Scope and Determine Skill Name), §4a/§6a (multi-scope), and §0a (docs-only) — wherever the skill name is assigned, append `{coexistence_suffix}` if non-empty.
  - [ ] 2.3 Continue to the standard flow (§0a for doc URLs, §1 for repo URLs). The existing skill is untouched.

- [ ] Task 3: Implement [M]erge path (AC: #3)
  - [ ] 3.1 When [M]erge is selected, emit an envelope signaling the forger to redirect to the US workflow for the matched skill. The envelope follows the existing pattern:
    ```
    SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"redirect","redirect_to":"US","skill_name":"{matched_skill_name}","skill_path":"{matched_active_path}","exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","coexistence":"merge"}
    ```
  - [ ] 3.2 Write the result contract per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md` with `status: "redirect"`.
  - [ ] 3.3 Chain to {nextStepFile} (health-check.md). The forger's pipeline logic receives the redirect envelope and routes to US instead of continuing to BS. **STOP HERE — do not proceed to §1 or §0a.**

- [ ] Task 4: Implement [S]kip path (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 4.1 When [S]kip is selected, emit a skip envelope:
    ```
    SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"skipped","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":1,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","coexistence":"skip","skipped_reason":"Existing skill for {matched_skill_name}"}
    ```
  - [ ] 4.2 Write the result contract with `status: "skipped"`.
  - [ ] 4.3 Chain to {nextStepFile} (health-check.md). **STOP HERE — do not proceed to §1 or §0a.**

- [ ] Task 5: Update AN SKILL.md documentation (AC: #1, #5, #6)
  - [ ] 5.1 In the Stages table, add a note to step 1a: "Includes §0c coexistence detection — checks for existing skills before proceeding"
  - [ ] 5.2 In the Auto mode path description, add: "After URL type detection (§0), coexistence detection (§0c) checks for existing skills matching the target. If found, the user chooses alongside/merge/skip. Headless mode auto-selects alongside."
  - [ ] 5.3 In the Exit Codes table, document: exit code 0 also covers the "skipped" and "redirect" statuses from coexistence detection.
  - [ ] 5.4 In the Result Contract section, document the new `coexistence` field in the envelope and the `redirect` and `skipped` status values.

- [ ] Task 6: Verify backward compatibility (AC: #5)
  - [ ] 6.1 Verify that when `{skills_output_folder}` is empty or doesn't exist, `skf-skill-inventory.py` returns gracefully and §0c skips silently — no error, no user prompt.
  - [ ] 6.2 Verify that the `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, and `maintain` pipeline aliases are unaffected (they don't pass `[auto]`, so step-auto-scope.md is never loaded).
  - [ ] 6.3 Verify that the interactive AN path (no `[auto]`) is unchanged — §0c is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true.
  - [ ] 6.4 Run `npm test` — full regression suite passes.

## Dev Notes

### Architecture: Coexistence as a Pre-Flight Gate in Auto-Scope

Story 2.6 adds coexistence detection as §0c in step-auto-scope.md — between URL type detection (§0) and the actual scope work (§0a docs-only or §1 repo-based). The change is a **conditional gate**: if existing skills match the target, the user decides; if not, the flow continues silently.

**Current flow (Story 2.5):**
```
§0 URL Type Detection
  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit → health-check
  └── repo URL → §1 → §2 → §3 → ... → §11
```

**New flow (Story 2.6):**
```
§0 URL Type Detection
§0c Coexistence Detection (all URL types)
  ├── No match → continue silently
  ├── [A]longside → set -wiki suffix, continue
  ├── [M]erge → emit redirect envelope → health-check (STOP)
  └── [S]kip → emit skip envelope → health-check (STOP)
Then (if continuing):
  ├── doc URL → §0a Docs-Only Short-Circuit → health-check
  └── repo URL → §1 → §2 → §3 → ... → §11
```

§0c is a gate, not a branch — it does not change what happens after it (docs-only vs repo), only whether the flow continues at all. The [A]longside path continues with a `-wiki` suffix; [M]erge and [S]kip terminate early.

[Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#FR→File Mapping — "FR-21 maps to src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md"]

### Inventory Script Interface

Coexistence detection invokes `skf-skill-inventory.py`, an existing shared script used by 9+ workflows. The script scans the skills output folder and returns structured JSON.

**Invocation:**
```bash
uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-skill-inventory.py {skills_output_folder}
```

**Output (relevant fields for matching):**
```json
{
  "status": "ok",
  "skills": [
    {
      "name": "some-library",
      "path": "/path/to/skill/group",
      "active_version": "1.0.0",
      "active_path": "/path/to/active/version",
      "metadata": {
        "source_repo": "https://github.com/org/some-library",
        "version": "1.0.0",
        "language": "typescript"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

**Exit codes:** 0 = success (may have zero skills), 1 = error (directory not found, etc.)

The script already exists at `src/shared/scripts/skf-skill-inventory.py`. No modifications needed to the script itself — it already returns `metadata.source_repo` which is the primary matching key.

[Source: src/shared/scripts/skf-skill-inventory.py — full script, 210 lines]

### Matching Logic

The matching must be robust against URL format variations:

| Target Input | Existing `source_repo` | Should Match? |
|---|---|---|
| `https://github.com/org/foo` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (exact) |
| `https://github.com/org/foo.git` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (strip `.git`) |
| `https://github.com/org/foo/` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (strip trailing `/`) |
| `https://GITHUB.com/ORG/foo` | `https://github.com/org/foo` | Yes (case-insensitive) |
| `https://github.com/org/foo` | `https://github.com/other-org/foo` | No (different org) |
| `https://docs.foo.io` | skill named `docs-foo-io` | Yes (name match for doc URLs) |

**Two match conditions (either triggers a hit):**
1. **URL match:** Normalize both the target and each skill's `source_repo` — strip scheme, strip trailing `.git`, strip trailing `/`, lowercase — then compare.
2. **Name match:** Derive the expected skill name from the target (same logic as §6 for repo URLs, §0a for doc URLs), then compare against each skill's `name`.

Both normalizations happen in the step file (string manipulation), not in a Python script — the logic is simple enough for step-file-level pattern matching.

### Coexistence Gate UI

When matches are found, present:

```
⚠️ Existing skill(s) found for {target_name}:

  • {skill_name} (v{version}) — source: {source_repo}
  [repeat for each match]

Actions:
  [A]longside — Create a new wiki skill with "-wiki" suffix (existing skill untouched)
  [M]erge     — Update the existing skill via US workflow (wiki data enriches it)
  [S]kip      — Do not create or modify any skill for this library

Choose [A/M/S]:
```

In headless mode, auto-select `[A]` and log the decision.

### -wiki Suffix Mechanics

When [A]longside is selected, the `{coexistence_suffix}` variable is set to `-wiki`. This suffix is consumed at skill name assignment points:

| Section | Current Name Assignment | With Suffix |
|---|---|---|
| §0a (docs-only) | `skill_name` = domain-derived (e.g., `docs-example-com`) | `docs-example-com-wiki` |
| §6 (single-scope) | `skill_name` = manifest name or directory name | `{name}-wiki` |
| §4a/§6a (multi-scope) | `skill_name` = `{project}-{package}` | `{project}-{package}-wiki` |

The suffix applies to:
- The `name` field in skill-brief.yaml
- The forge-data subdirectory name (`{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}-wiki/`)
- The analysis report `confirmed_units[].name`

The suffix does NOT apply to:
- The `source_repo` field (still points to the original repo)
- Include/exclude patterns (still scope the original source)
- The shape detection or scope mapping (shape is a property of the source, not the skill)

### [M]erge Path: Redirect to US

The [M]erge path introduces a new `redirect` status in the AN envelope. The forger's pipeline logic already handles status values — it checks for `"success"` and `"error"`. A `"redirect"` status signals the forger to substitute the next workflow in the chain.

**Forger behavior on receiving `status: "redirect"`:**
- The `redirect_to` field names the target workflow code (`US`)
- The `skill_name` field identifies which skill US should operate on
- The forger replaces the remaining pipeline chain with `US TS EX` (update → test → export)
- This is analogous to the existing `AS → US` skip logic: "if severity is CLEAN, skip US"

The redirect is a **step-file-level instruction** — step-auto-scope.md emits the envelope, and the forger's existing pipeline orchestration interprets it. No new forger code is needed beyond recognizing `"redirect"` in the status field.

**Important:** The [M]erge path does NOT directly invoke US. It emits the redirect envelope and chains to health-check. The forger reads the envelope and handles routing. This preserves the pipeline's left-to-right, one-workflow-at-a-time model.

[Source: src/shared/references/pipeline-contracts.md — "Data Flow" table, forger routes based on result status]
[Source: src/skf-forger/SKILL.md:105 — "AS followed by US: if summary.severity is CLEAN, skip US"]

### [S]kip Path: Clean Termination

The [S]kip path uses the existing `"skipped"` status pattern (analogous to how the forger skips US when AS reports CLEAN). The envelope `unit_counts.skipped: 1` signals that one unit was intentionally skipped. The forger treats this as a successful termination — no error, no downstream workflows executed.

### Headless Gate Convention

Per the established convention (`headless-gate-convention.md`, stories 2.3/2.5):
- Coexistence gate is a **Confirm Gate [C]** with a default action
- Default action: `[A]longside` (safest — creates new skill, preserves existing)
- In headless mode: auto-select default and log the auto-decision
- The gate does NOT auto-select [M]erge (destructive — modifies existing) or [S]kip (lossy — produces nothing)

[Source: src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md — headless gate pattern reference]

- No new scripts — `skf-skill-inventory.py` already exists and returns all needed data
- No new test files — step files are validated structurally via `npm test`, not with pytest
- The matching logic (URL normalization + name comparison) is step-file-level string manipulation, not a Python script

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

1. **Do NOT modify `skf-skill-inventory.py`.** The script already returns all needed fields. Adding coexistence-specific logic to the shared script would couple it to the deepwiki pipeline. Matching logic belongs in the step file.

2. **Do NOT invoke US directly from step-auto-scope.md.** The [M]erge path emits a redirect envelope — the forger handles routing. Direct invocation would break the pipeline's left-to-right model.

3. **Do NOT overwrite existing skills in any path.** [A]longside creates a new skill with `-wiki` suffix. [M]erge delegates to US. [S]kip does nothing. The entire point of FR-21 is "never silently overwrite."

4. **Do NOT run coexistence detection in interactive AN mode.** §0c is only executed when `{auto_mode}` is true (i.e., within the deepwiki pipeline). Interactive AN users manually choose their targets.

5. **Do NOT make coexistence detection blocking when inventory fails.** If `skf-skill-inventory.py` returns an error (skills dir doesn't exist, JSON parse error), skip detection silently. This is a pre-flight check, not a hard gate — failure to check is not a reason to halt the pipeline.

6. **Do NOT add the `-wiki` suffix to the `source_repo` field.** The source repo is the actual repo/URL. The suffix only applies to the skill name and derived paths.

7. **Do NOT renumber existing sections (§0, §0a, §1-§11).** Insert §0c as a new section without changing the numbering of existing sections. This matches the pattern from stories 2.4 and 2.5.

8	
9	# Step 1a: Auto-Scope Analysis
10	
11	## STEP GOAL:
12	
13	To automatically scope a repo using shape detection and export surface analysis, producing a scope and skill-brief.yaml without requiring manual input. This step replaces the interactive scan-project → identify-units → map-and-detect → recommend → generate-briefs chain when `{auto_mode}` is true.
14	
15	## Rules
16	
17	- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
18	- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
19	- Must produce the same output artifacts as the interactive chain: analysis report + skill-brief.yaml
20	- On unknown shape (exit code 1), fall back to `scan-project.md` (the normal interactive entry point)
21	- On error (exit code 2), HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`)
22	
23	## MANDATORY SEQUENCE
24	
25	### 0. URL Type Detection
26	
27	Read the target URL or path from the pipeline context (`{project_path}` or the first entry in `project_paths[]`).
28	
29	Apply the following heuristic to classify the input:
30	
31	| Input Pattern | Classification | Route |
32	|---------------|---------------|-------|
33	| `github.com/{owner}/{repo}` (with or without `.git` suffix, with or without scheme prefix) | GitHub repo | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
34	| `gitlab.com/...`, `bitbucket.org/...` | Git hosting | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
35	| Starts with `/`, `./`, `~/`, or `~` | Local filesystem path | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
36	| Any other `https://` or `http://` URL | Documentation URL | §0a (docs-only) |
37	| Anything else (SSH URLs, `git://`, bare hostnames, etc.) | Unclassified | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
38	
39	If the input is classified as a documentation URL, continue to §0a.
40	
41	For all other classifications (repo URL, local path, or unclassified), continue to §1 without further action.
42	
43	### 0a. Docs-Only Short-Circuit
44	
45	This section handles documentation URLs that are not GitHub repos or local paths. It validates the URL, writes a minimal brief and analysis report, emits the envelope, and chains directly to health-check — skipping §1 through §11 entirely.
46	
47	**1. Validate URL reachability:**
48	
49	```bash
50	curl -sI --max-time 5 {url}
51	```
52	
53	- On **2xx/3xx** response: URL is reachable. Continue.
54	- On **4xx/5xx**, DNS failure, or timeout: HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit error message: `"Documentation URL unreachable: {url} — {status or error}"`. Emit error envelope:
55	  ```
56	  SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":3,"halt_reason":"path-invalid","mode":"auto","source_type":"docs-only"}
57	  ```
58	
59	**2. Derive skill name from URL domain:**
60	
61	Extract the hostname from the URL (e.g., `docs.example.com` from `https://docs.example.com/guide/intro`), convert to kebab-case (replace `.` with `-`), yielding e.g. `docs-example-com`.
62	
63	**3. Write analysis report:**
64	
65	Update {outputFile} with docs-only results.
66	
67	**Update frontmatter:**
68	```yaml
69	stepsCompleted: ['init', 'auto-scope']
70	lastStep: 'auto-scope'
71	source_type: docs-only
72	confirmed_units:
73	  - name: '{skill_name}'
74	    shape: 'docs-only'
75	    confidence: 1.0
76	    export_count: 0
77	    package_count: 0
78	```
79	
80	**Append body section:**
81	```markdown
82	## Auto-Scope Analysis
83	
84	**Mode:** auto (docs-only short-circuit)
85	**Source Type:** docs-only
86	**Documentation URL:** {url}
87	**Skill Name:** {skill_name}
88	```
89	
90	**4. Write skill brief via canonical writer:**
91	
92	Create directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` if it does not exist.
93	
94	Write `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` through the canonical writer (`skf-write-skill-brief.py`) with the following context:
95	
96	```json
97	{
98	  "name":             "{skill_name}",
99	  "target_version":   null,
100	  "detected_version": null,
101	  "source_type":      "docs-only",
102	  "source_repo":      "{url}",
103	  "language":         "",
104	  "description":      "Skill created from documentation at {url}",
105	  "forge_tier":       "{forge_tier}",
106	  "created":          "{current_date}",
107	  "created_by":       "{user_name}",
108	  "scope_type":       "docs-only",
109	  "scope_include":    [],
110	  "scope_exclude":    [],
111	  "scope_notes":      "Docs-only skill created from documentation URL",
112	  "scope_rationale":  null,
113	  "scope_tier_a_include": null,
114	  "scope_amendments":     null,
115	  "doc_urls":         [{"url": "{url}", "label": "Primary Documentation"}],
116	  "scripts_intent":   null,
117	  "assets_intent":    null,
118	  "source_authority": "community",
119	  "target_ref":       null,
120	  "source_ref":       null,
121	  "version_resolved": "1.0.0"
122	}
123	```
124	
125	**5. Emit success envelope:**
126	
127	```
128	SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"{outputFile_path}","brief_paths":["{brief_path}"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":1,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","source_type":"docs-only"}
129	```
130	
131	The `source_type` field signals downstream consumers (BS) to skip repo-based enrichment.
132	
133	**6. Write result contract** per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md`: the per-run record and latest copy, same as §10.
134	
135	If `{onCompleteCommand}` is non-empty, invoke it now with `--result-path={result_json_path}`.
136	
137	**7. Chain to health check:**
138	
139	Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check. **Skip §1 through §11 entirely.**
140	
141	### 1. Load Context
142	
143	Read {outputFile} frontmatter to obtain:
144	- `project_paths[]` — the root(s) to analyze
145	- `forge_tier` — for brief generation
146	- `project_name`, `user_name`, `date`
147	
148	Load `references/step-shape-detect.md` as reference for shape detection invocation contract and shape→scope mapping.
149	
150	### 2. Manifest Scan
151	
152	Perform a lightweight manifest scan — find standard package manifests in the project root and workspace paths. Do NOT crawl the full directory tree.
153	
154	**For each path in `project_paths[]`:**
155	
156	1. Check the project root for: `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `Cargo.toml`
157	2. If a workspace configuration exists (e.g., `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, Cargo.toml `[workspace].members`), scan workspace member paths for additional manifests
158	3. Record each discovered manifest as `{path, type}` pairs
159	
160	**IF no manifests found:**
161	- Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not find any package manifests — switching to interactive mode.**"
162	- Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**
163	
164	### 3. Invoke Shape Detection
165	
166	Invoke the shape detection script with discovered manifests:
167	
168	```
169	uv run python {shapeDetectScript} --repo-url <project_path_or_url> --manifests <comma_separated_manifest_paths>
170	```
171	
172	Parse the JSON output: `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}`
173	
174	**Handle exit codes:**
175	
176	- **Exit 0 (shape classified):** Continue to §3a.
177	- **Exit 1 (unknown shape):** Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not classify this repo — switching to interactive mode.**" Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**
178	- **Exit 2 (error):** HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit the error envelope:
179	  ```
180	  SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":3,"halt_reason":"resolution-failure","mode":"auto"}
181	  ```
182	
183	### 3a. Check Decomposition Thresholds
184	
185	Evaluate the shape detection output to determine whether this repo should be decomposed into multiple skills.
186	
187	**Threshold conditions** (both constants are `[PENDING VALIDATION]` — subject to empirical tuning):
188	
189	| Threshold | Condition | Signal |
190	|-----------|-----------|--------|
191	| Large export surface | `export_count > 500` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | Single skill covering 500+ exports produces unwieldy output |
192	| Monorepo / multi-package | `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | 4+ packages are almost always multi-concern |
193	
194	**Decision:**
195	
196	- **Neither threshold met** → Continue to §4 (single-scope flow, entirely unchanged).
197	- **Either or both thresholds met** → Route to §4a (multi-scope decomposition flow). Log: "Auto-decomposition triggered: {reason} ({value} exceeds threshold {threshold} `[PENDING VALIDATION]`)" where reason is `export_threshold`, `package_threshold`, or `both`.
198	
199	### 4. Map Shape to Scope
200	
201	Apply the shape→scope.type mapping:
202	
203	| Shape (from skf-shape-detect.py) | scope.type | Condition |
204	|----------------------------------|------------|-----------|
205	| `library-API` | `full-library` | export_count ≤ 200 |
206	| `library-API` | `public-api` | export_count > 200 |
207	| `reference-app` | `reference-app` | — |
208	| `language-reference` | `full-library` | — |
209	| `stack-compose` | `full-library` | Multi-scope via §3a when `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` |
210	
211	### 5. Generate Include/Exclude Patterns
212	
213	Generate `scope.include` and `scope.exclude` arrays from the detected language and project structure.
214	
215	**Detect primary language** from manifest type:
216	- `package.json` → TypeScript/JavaScript
217	- `pyproject.toml` → Python
218	- `Cargo.toml` → Rust
219	- `go.mod` → Go
220	
221	**Default patterns (adjust based on actual project structure):**
222	
223	| Language | Default include | Default exclude |
224	|----------|-----------------|-----------------|
225	| TypeScript/JavaScript | `['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx']` | `['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.spec.ts', '**/node_modules/**']` |
226	| Python | `['src/**/*.py']` or `['{package_name}/**/*.py']` | `['**/*_test.py', '**/test_*.py', '**/tests/**']` |
227	| Rust | `['src/**/*.rs']` | `['**/tests/**', '**/benches/**']` |
228	
229	**Adjust for actual layout:** If the project uses a non-standard layout (e.g., `lib/` instead of `src/`, or a named package directory for Python), detect and use the actual paths. Check for the existence of common source directories (`src/`, `lib/`, `pkg/`, the package name directory) and prefer the one that exists.
230	
231	### 6. Build Scope and Determine Skill Name
232	
233	Build the scope object:
234	```yaml
235	scope:
236	  type: '{mapped_scope_type}'
237	  include: ['{generated_include_patterns}']
238	  exclude: ['{generated_exclude_patterns}']
239	  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
240	```
241	
242	Determine the skill name from the project name or package name (kebab-case, lowercase). Use the manifest `name` field if available, otherwise derive from the project directory name.
243	
244	Detect the primary language from the manifest ecosystem:
245	- `npm` → `typescript` (or `javascript` if no `.ts` files in includes)
246	- `python` → `python`
247	- `rust` → `rust`
248	
249	### 4a. Multi-Scope Decomposition
250	
251	This section is reached only from §3a when one or both decomposition thresholds are met. It replaces §4→§5→§6 for repos that will produce N > 1 skills.
252	
253	**Determine decomposition path:**
254	
255	- **Monorepo path** (`package_count > 3`): Use workspace package discovery from §2 manifest scan results. Each workspace package with its own manifest becomes a separate skill boundary. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{package_name}` (kebab-case). Trivial workspace members (no source files, no exports) are excluded.
256	- **Large-export path** (`export_count > 500`, single package): Group by top-level source directory modules (e.g., `src/auth/`, `src/core/`, `src/api/`). Each directory subtree with a meaningful export surface becomes a separate skill boundary. Candidate boundaries with fewer than ~50 exports `[PENDING VALIDATION]` should be merged into an "other" catch-all skill rather than becoming standalone skills. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{module_name}` (kebab-case). If no clear module structure exists (flat `src/` with all files at root level), **do not force decomposition** — fall back to single-scope flow at §4.
257	- **Combined path** (both thresholds met): Use the monorepo path. Package boundaries are explicit and take priority over export-count grouping (which is heuristic).
258	
259	**Per-boundary shape→scope mapping:**
260	
261	For each decomposed boundary, apply the shape→scope mapping from §4 independently — the boundary's local characteristics determine its scope.type:
262	
263	| Decomposition Type | Per-Boundary Shape Mapping |
264	|--------------------|---------------------------|
265	| Monorepo (`package_count > 3`) | Re-run the shape→scope heuristic ladder from `step-shape-detect.md` per package using each package's own manifest data. Packages may have different shapes (e.g., a `library-API` core + a `reference-app` CLI). |
266	| Large-export (`export_count > 500`) | All boundaries inherit the parent shape. scope.type varies by per-boundary export count (e.g., ≤200 → `full-library`, >200 → `public-api`). |
267	
268	### 5a. Generate Multi-Scope Patterns
269	
270	For each decomposed boundary, generate include/exclude patterns using the same language-aware rules as §5, but scoped to the boundary's source paths.
271	
272	- Monorepo boundaries: patterns are rooted at the package path (e.g., `packages/auth/src/**/*.ts` instead of `src/**/*.ts`)
273	- Large-export boundaries: patterns are rooted at the module directory (e.g., `src/auth/**/*.ts`)
274	
275	### 6a. Build Multi-Scope
276	
277	For each boundary, build a scope object following the same structure as §6.
278	
279	Include decomposition metadata in `scope.notes`: "Decomposed from {project_name} — boundary {i}/{N} ({reason})"
280	
281	Determine each boundary's skill name from the boundary-derived name (kebab-case, lowercase). Detect the primary language from each boundary's manifest ecosystem (same rules as §6).
282	
283	After building all N scopes, continue to §7 with the full set of boundaries.
284	
285	### 7. Write Analysis Report
286	
287	Update {outputFile} with auto-scope results.
288	
289	**Update frontmatter:**
290	```yaml
291	stepsCompleted: ['init', 'auto-scope']
292	lastStep: 'auto-scope'
293	confirmed_units:
294	  - name: '{skill_name}'
295	    shape: '{shape}'
296	    confidence: {confidence}
297	    export_count: {export_count}
298	    package_count: {package_count}
299	    boundary_path: '{boundary_path}'  # present only for decomposed units
300	  # ... N entries when decomposition is active
301	```
302	
303	**When decomposition was triggered (N > 1 units):**
304	
305	Add `decomposition` to frontmatter:
306	```yaml
307	decomposition:
308	  triggered: true
309	  reason: 'export_threshold' | 'package_threshold' | 'both'
310	  boundary_count: N
311	```
312	
313	Each `confirmed_units` entry includes `boundary_path` — the relative path to the boundary's root (e.g., `packages/core` for monorepo, `src/auth` for large-export). Omit the `decomposition` key entirely when single-scope (N = 1).
314	
315	**When single-scope (N = 1):** No `decomposition` key. `confirmed_units` contains a single entry (existing behavior).
316	
317	**Append body section:**
318	
319	For single-scope (unchanged):
320	```markdown
321	## Auto-Scope Analysis
322	
323	**Mode:** auto
324	**Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
325	**Signals:** {signals list}
326	**Export Count:** {export_count}
327	**Package Count:** {package_count}
328	**Resolved Scope Type:** {scope_type}
329	**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
330	**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
331	```
332	
333	For multi-scope (N > 1):
334	```markdown
335	## Auto-Scope Analysis — Decomposition ({N} skills)
336	
337	**Mode:** auto
338	**Decomposition:** {reason} ({N} boundaries)
339	**Parent Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
340	**Export Count:** {export_count}
341	**Package Count:** {package_count}
342	
343	### Boundary 1: {boundary_name}
344	**Scope Type:** {scope_type}
345	**Boundary Path:** {boundary_path}
346	**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
347	**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
348	**Rationale:** {boundary_rationale}
349	
350	### Boundary 2: {boundary_name}
351	...
352	```
353	
354	### 8. Write Skill Brief
355	
356	**For each confirmed unit** (1 for single-scope, N for decomposition):
357	
358	Create directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` if it does not exist.
359	
360	Write `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` conforming to the skill-brief schema (`assets/skill-brief-schema.md`):
361	
362	```yaml
363	name: '{skill_name}'
364	version: '{detected_version or 1.0.0}'
365	source_repo: '{project_path}'
366	language: '{detected_language}'
367	scope:
368	  type: '{scope_type}'
369	  include:
370	    - '{include_patterns}'
371	  exclude:
372	    - '{exclude_patterns}'
373	  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
374	description: '{1-3 sentence description based on shape, language, and manifest name}'
375	forge_tier: '{forge_tier}'
376	created: '{current_date}'
377	created_by: '{user_name}'
378	```
379	
380	**When decomposition is active (N > 1 units):**
381	
382	Loop over all N boundaries. For each boundary:
383	- `name` is the boundary-derived skill name (e.g., `my-monorepo-core`)
384	- `include`/`exclude` patterns are boundary-scoped (from §5a)
385	- `scope.notes` includes decomposition context: "Decomposed from {project_name} ({N} skills) — boundary {i}/{N}: {boundary_description}"
386	- `description` references the parent project and boundary role (e.g., "Core library package of the my-monorepo project, providing...")
387	- All N briefs share the same `version`, `source_repo`, `language`, `forge_tier`, `created`, `created_by` values as the parent project
388	
389	**Version detection:** Attempt to auto-detect the source version per the version detection rules in `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`. Fall back to `1.0.0` if detection fails.
390	
391	### 9. Emit Result Envelope
392	
393	Emit the `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` envelope on stdout:
394	
395	```
396	SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"{outputFile_path}","brief_paths":["{brief_path_1}","{brief_path_2}",...,"{brief_path_N}"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":N,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}
397	```
398	
399	`brief_paths` contains N paths (one per confirmed unit). `unit_counts.confirmed` is N. The envelope JSON format is structurally unchanged — `brief_paths` was already an array and `unit_counts.confirmed` was already a number. No breaking change for downstream consumers.
400	
401	### 10. Write Result Contract
402	
403	Write the result contract per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md`: the per-run record at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json` (UTC timestamp, resolution to seconds) and a copy at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-latest.json`. `outputs` lists all N brief paths and `summary` includes the brief count N.
404	
405	If `{onCompleteCommand}` is non-empty, invoke it now with `--result-path={result_json_path}`.
406	
407	### 11. Chain to Health Check
408	
409	Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check.
410	
411	## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE
412	
413	ONLY WHEN the analysis report has been updated, the skill-brief.yaml written and validated, the result envelope emitted, and the result contract saved will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the health check.
414

file.content:
---
nextStepFile: 'health-check.md'
outputFile: '{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-report-{project_name}.md'
shapeDetectScript: 'src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py'
---

<!-- Config: communicate in {communication_language}. -->

# Step 1a: Auto-Scope Analysis

## STEP GOAL:

To automatically scope a repo using shape detection and export surface analysis, producing a scope and skill-brief.yaml without requiring manual input. This step replaces the interactive scan-project → identify-units → map-and-detect → recommend → generate-briefs chain when `{auto_mode}` is true.

## Rules

- Auto-proceed step — no user interaction required
- This step is conditional — only loaded when `[auto]` flag is present in the pipeline context
- Must produce the same output artifacts as the interactive chain: analysis report + skill-brief.yaml
- On unknown shape (exit code 1), fall back to `scan-project.md` (the normal interactive entry point)
- On error (exit code 2), HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`)

## MANDATORY SEQUENCE

### 0. URL Type Detection

Read the target URL or path from the pipeline context (`{project_path}` or the first entry in `project_paths[]`).

Apply the following heuristic to classify the input:

| Input Pattern | Classification | Route |
|---------------|---------------|-------|
| `github.com/{owner}/{repo}` (with or without `.git` suffix, with or without scheme prefix) | GitHub repo | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
| `gitlab.com/...`, `bitbucket.org/...` | Git hosting | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
| Starts with `/`, `./`, `~/`, or `~` | Local filesystem path | §1 (standard auto-scope) |
| Any other `https://` or `http://` URL | Documentation URL | §0a (docs-only) |
| Anything else (SSH URLs, `git://`, bare hostnames, etc.) | Unclassified | §1 (standard auto-scope) |

If the input is classified as a documentation URL, continue to §0a.

For all other classifications (repo URL, local path, or unclassified), continue to §1 without further action.

### 0a. Docs-Only Short-Circuit

This section handles documentation URLs that are not GitHub repos or local paths. It validates the URL, writes a minimal brief and analysis report, emits the envelope, and chains directly to health-check — skipping §1 through §11 entirely.

**1. Validate URL reachability:**

```bash
curl -sI --max-time 5 {url}
```

- On **2xx/3xx** response: URL is reachable. Continue.
- On **4xx/5xx**, DNS failure, or timeout: HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit error message: `"Documentation URL unreachable: {url} — {status or error}"`. Emit error envelope:
  ```
  SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":3,"halt_reason":"path-invalid","mode":"auto","source_type":"docs-only"}
  ```

**2. Derive skill name from URL domain:**

Extract the hostname from the URL (e.g., `docs.example.com` from `https://docs.example.com/guide/intro`), convert to kebab-case (replace `.` with `-`), yielding e.g. `docs-example-com`.

**3. Write analysis report:**

Update {outputFile} with docs-only results.

**Update frontmatter:**
```yaml
stepsCompleted: ['init', 'auto-scope']
lastStep: 'auto-scope'
source_type: docs-only
confirmed_units:
  - name: '{skill_name}'
    shape: 'docs-only'
    confidence: 1.0
    export_count: 0
    package_count: 0
```

**Append body section:**
```markdown
## Auto-Scope Analysis

**Mode:** auto (docs-only short-circuit)
**Source Type:** docs-only
**Documentation URL:** {url}
**Skill Name:** {skill_name}
```

**4. Write skill brief via canonical writer:**

Create directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` if it does not exist.

Write `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` through the canonical writer (`skf-write-skill-brief.py`) with the following context:

```json
{
  "name":             "{skill_name}",
  "target_version":   null,
  "detected_version": null,
  "source_type":      "docs-only",
  "source_repo":      "{url}",
  "language":         "",
  "description":      "Skill created from documentation at {url}",
  "forge_tier":       "{forge_tier}",
  "created":          "{current_date}",
  "created_by":       "{user_name}",
  "scope_type":       "docs-only",
  "scope_include":    [],
  "scope_exclude":    [],
  "scope_notes":      "Docs-only skill created from documentation URL",
  "scope_rationale":  null,
  "scope_tier_a_include": null,
  "scope_amendments":     null,
  "doc_urls":         [{"url": "{url}", "label": "Primary Documentation"}],
  "scripts_intent":   null,
  "assets_intent":    null,
  "source_authority": "community",
  "target_ref":       null,
  "source_ref":       null,
  "version_resolved": "1.0.0"
}
```

**5. Emit success envelope:**

```
SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"{outputFile_path}","brief_paths":["{brief_path}"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":1,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto","source_type":"docs-only"}
```

The `source_type` field signals downstream consumers (BS) to skip repo-based enrichment.

**6. Write result contract** per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md`: the per-run record and latest copy, same as §10.

If `{onCompleteCommand}` is non-empty, invoke it now with `--result-path={result_json_path}`.

**7. Chain to health check:**

Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check. **Skip §1 through §11 entirely.**

### 1. Load Context

Read {outputFile} frontmatter to obtain:
- `project_paths[]` — the root(s) to analyze
- `forge_tier` — for brief generation
- `project_name`, `user_name`, `date`

Load `references/step-shape-detect.md` as reference for shape detection invocation contract and shape→scope mapping.

### 2. Manifest Scan

Perform a lightweight manifest scan — find standard package manifests in the project root and workspace paths. Do NOT crawl the full directory tree.

**For each path in `project_paths[]`:**

1. Check the project root for: `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `Cargo.toml`
2. If a workspace configuration exists (e.g., `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, Cargo.toml `[workspace].members`), scan workspace member paths for additional manifests
3. Record each discovered manifest as `{path, type}` pairs

**IF no manifests found:**
- Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not find any package manifests — switching to interactive mode.**"
- Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**

### 3. Invoke Shape Detection

Invoke the shape detection script with discovered manifests:

```
uv run python {shapeDetectScript} --repo-url <project_path_or_url> --manifests <comma_separated_manifest_paths>
```

Parse the JSON output: `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}`

**Handle exit codes:**

- **Exit 0 (shape classified):** Continue to §3a.
- **Exit 1 (unknown shape):** Emit fallback message: "**Auto-scope could not classify this repo — switching to interactive mode.**" Load, read fully, then execute `references/scan-project.md`. **STOP HERE.**
- **Exit 2 (error):** HARD HALT with exit code 3 (`resolution-failure`). Emit the error envelope:
  ```
  SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"error","report_path":null,"brief_paths":[],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":0,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":3,"halt_reason":"resolution-failure","mode":"auto"}
  ```

### 3a. Check Decomposition Thresholds

Evaluate the shape detection output to determine whether this repo should be decomposed into multiple skills.

**Threshold conditions** (both constants are `[PENDING VALIDATION]` — subject to empirical tuning):

| Threshold | Condition | Signal |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| Large export surface | `export_count > 500` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | Single skill covering 500+ exports produces unwieldy output |
| Monorepo / multi-package | `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` | 4+ packages are almost always multi-concern |

**Decision:**

- **Neither threshold met** → Continue to §4 (single-scope flow, entirely unchanged).
- **Either or both thresholds met** → Route to §4a (multi-scope decomposition flow). Log: "Auto-decomposition triggered: {reason} ({value} exceeds threshold {threshold} `[PENDING VALIDATION]`)" where reason is `export_threshold`, `package_threshold`, or `both`.

### 4. Map Shape to Scope

Apply the shape→scope.type mapping:

| Shape (from skf-shape-detect.py) | scope.type | Condition |
|----------------------------------|------------|-----------|
| `library-API` | `full-library` | export_count ≤ 200 |
| `library-API` | `public-api` | export_count > 200 |
| `reference-app` | `reference-app` | — |
| `language-reference` | `full-library` | — |
| `stack-compose` | `full-library` | Multi-scope via §3a when `package_count > 3` `[PENDING VALIDATION]` |

### 5. Generate Include/Exclude Patterns

Generate `scope.include` and `scope.exclude` arrays from the detected language and project structure.

**Detect primary language** from manifest type:
- `package.json` → TypeScript/JavaScript
- `pyproject.toml` → Python
- `Cargo.toml` → Rust
- `go.mod` → Go

**Default patterns (adjust based on actual project structure):**

| Language | Default include | Default exclude |
|----------|-----------------|-----------------|
| TypeScript/JavaScript | `['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx']` | `['**/*.test.ts', '**/*.spec.ts', '**/node_modules/**']` |
| Python | `['src/**/*.py']` or `['{package_name}/**/*.py']` | `['**/*_test.py', '**/test_*.py', '**/tests/**']` |
| Rust | `['src/**/*.rs']` | `['**/tests/**', '**/benches/**']` |

**Adjust for actual layout:** If the project uses a non-standard layout (e.g., `lib/` instead of `src/`, or a named package directory for Python), detect and use the actual paths. Check for the existence of common source directories (`src/`, `lib/`, `pkg/`, the package name directory) and prefer the one that exists.

### 6. Build Scope and Determine Skill Name

Build the scope object:
```yaml
scope:
  type: '{mapped_scope_type}'
  include: ['{generated_include_patterns}']
  exclude: ['{generated_exclude_patterns}']
  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
```

Determine the skill name from the project name or package name (kebab-case, lowercase). Use the manifest `name` field if available, otherwise derive from the project directory name.

Detect the primary language from the manifest ecosystem:
- `npm` → `typescript` (or `javascript` if no `.ts` files in includes)
- `python` → `python`
- `rust` → `rust`

### 4a. Multi-Scope Decomposition

This section is reached only from §3a when one or both decomposition thresholds are met. It replaces §4→§5→§6 for repos that will produce N > 1 skills.

**Determine decomposition path:**

- **Monorepo path** (`package_count > 3`): Use workspace package discovery from §2 manifest scan results. Each workspace package with its own manifest becomes a separate skill boundary. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{package_name}` (kebab-case). Trivial workspace members (no source files, no exports) are excluded.
- **Large-export path** (`export_count > 500`, single package): Group by top-level source directory modules (e.g., `src/auth/`, `src/core/`, `src/api/`). Each directory subtree with a meaningful export surface becomes a separate skill boundary. Candidate boundaries with fewer than ~50 exports `[PENDING VALIDATION]` should be merged into an "other" catch-all skill rather than becoming standalone skills. Name each skill as `{project_name}-{module_name}` (kebab-case). If no clear module structure exists (flat `src/` with all files at root level), **do not force decomposition** — fall back to single-scope flow at §4.
- **Combined path** (both thresholds met): Use the monorepo path. Package boundaries are explicit and take priority over export-count grouping (which is heuristic).

**Per-boundary shape→scope mapping:**

For each decomposed boundary, apply the shape→scope mapping from §4 independently — the boundary's local characteristics determine its scope.type:

| Decomposition Type | Per-Boundary Shape Mapping |
|--------------------|---------------------------|
| Monorepo (`package_count > 3`) | Re-run the shape→scope heuristic ladder from `step-shape-detect.md` per package using each package's own manifest data. Packages may have different shapes (e.g., a `library-API` core + a `reference-app` CLI). |
| Large-export (`export_count > 500`) | All boundaries inherit the parent shape. scope.type varies by per-boundary export count (e.g., ≤200 → `full-library`, >200 → `public-api`). |

### 5a. Generate Multi-Scope Patterns

For each decomposed boundary, generate include/exclude patterns using the same language-aware rules as §5, but scoped to the boundary's source paths.

- Monorepo boundaries: patterns are rooted at the package path (e.g., `packages/auth/src/**/*.ts` instead of `src/**/*.ts`)
- Large-export boundaries: patterns are rooted at the module directory (e.g., `src/auth/**/*.ts`)

### 6a. Build Multi-Scope

For each boundary, build a scope object following the same structure as §6.

Include decomposition metadata in `scope.notes`: "Decomposed from {project_name} — boundary {i}/{N} ({reason})"

Determine each boundary's skill name from the boundary-derived name (kebab-case, lowercase). Detect the primary language from each boundary's manifest ecosystem (same rules as §6).

After building all N scopes, continue to §7 with the full set of boundaries.

### 7. Write Analysis Report

Update {outputFile} with auto-scope results.

**Update frontmatter:**
```yaml
stepsCompleted: ['init', 'auto-scope']
lastStep: 'auto-scope'
confirmed_units:
  - name: '{skill_name}'
    shape: '{shape}'
    confidence: {confidence}
    export_count: {export_count}
    package_count: {package_count}
    boundary_path: '{boundary_path}'  # present only for decomposed units
  # ... N entries when decomposition is active
```

**When decomposition was triggered (N > 1 units):**

Add `decomposition` to frontmatter:
```yaml
decomposition:
  triggered: true
  reason: 'export_threshold' | 'package_threshold' | 'both'
  boundary_count: N
```

Each `confirmed_units` entry includes `boundary_path` — the relative path to the boundary's root (e.g., `packages/core` for monorepo, `src/auth` for large-export). Omit the `decomposition` key entirely when single-scope (N = 1).

**When single-scope (N = 1):** No `decomposition` key. `confirmed_units` contains a single entry (existing behavior).

**Append body section:**

For single-scope (unchanged):
```markdown
## Auto-Scope Analysis

**Mode:** auto
**Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
**Signals:** {signals list}
**Export Count:** {export_count}
**Package Count:** {package_count}
**Resolved Scope Type:** {scope_type}
**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
```

For multi-scope (N > 1):
```markdown
## Auto-Scope Analysis — Decomposition ({N} skills)

**Mode:** auto
**Decomposition:** {reason} ({N} boundaries)
**Parent Shape:** {shape} (confidence: {confidence})
**Export Count:** {export_count}
**Package Count:** {package_count}

### Boundary 1: {boundary_name}
**Scope Type:** {scope_type}
**Boundary Path:** {boundary_path}
**Include Patterns:** {include patterns}
**Exclude Patterns:** {exclude patterns}
**Rationale:** {boundary_rationale}

### Boundary 2: {boundary_name}
...
```

### 8. Write Skill Brief

**For each confirmed unit** (1 for single-scope, N for decomposition):

Create directory `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/` if it does not exist.

Write `{forge_data_folder}/{skill_name}/skill-brief.yaml` conforming to the skill-brief schema (`assets/skill-brief-schema.md`):

```yaml
name: '{skill_name}'
version: '{detected_version or 1.0.0}'
source_repo: '{project_path}'
language: '{detected_language}'
scope:
  type: '{scope_type}'
  include:
    - '{include_patterns}'
  exclude:
    - '{exclude_patterns}'
  notes: 'Auto-scoped from shape detection (shape: {shape}, confidence: {confidence})'
description: '{1-3 sentence description based on shape, language, and manifest name}'
forge_tier: '{forge_tier}'
created: '{current_date}'
created_by: '{user_name}'
```

**When decomposition is active (N > 1 units):**

Loop over all N boundaries. For each boundary:
- `name` is the boundary-derived skill name (e.g., `my-monorepo-core`)
- `include`/`exclude` patterns are boundary-scoped (from §5a)
- `scope.notes` includes decomposition context: "Decomposed from {project_name} ({N} skills) — boundary {i}/{N}: {boundary_description}"
- `description` references the parent project and boundary role (e.g., "Core library package of the my-monorepo project, providing...")
- All N briefs share the same `version`, `source_repo`, `language`, `forge_tier`, `created`, `created_by` values as the parent project

**Version detection:** Attempt to auto-detect the source version per the version detection rules in `assets/skill-brief-schema.md`. Fall back to `1.0.0` if detection fails.

### 9. Emit Result Envelope

Emit the `SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON` envelope on stdout:

```
SKF_ANALYZE_RESULT_JSON: {"status":"success","report_path":"{outputFile_path}","brief_paths":["{brief_path_1}","{brief_path_2}",...,"{brief_path_N}"],"unit_counts":{"confirmed":N,"skipped":0,"maybe":0},"exit_code":0,"halt_reason":null,"mode":"auto"}
```

`brief_paths` contains N paths (one per confirmed unit). `unit_counts.confirmed` is N. The envelope JSON format is structurally unchanged — `brief_paths` was already an array and `unit_counts.confirmed` was already a number. No breaking change for downstream consumers.

### 10. Write Result Contract

Write the result contract per `shared/references/output-contract-schema.md`: the per-run record at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-{YYYYMMDD-HHmmss}.json` (UTC timestamp, resolution to seconds) and a copy at `{forge_data_folder}/analyze-source-result-latest.json`. `outputs` lists all N brief paths and `summary` includes the brief count N.

If `{onCompleteCommand}` is non-empty, invoke it now with `--result-path={result_json_path}`.

### 11. Chain to Health Check

Load, read fully, then execute {nextStepFile} to run the shared workflow health check.

## CRITICAL STEP COMPLETION NOTE

ONLY WHEN the analysis report has been updated, the skill-brief.yaml written and validated, the result envelope emitted, and the result contract saved will you load and read fully {nextStepFile} to begin the health check.
